name: NemoClaw Architecture
description: NemoClaw is an NVIDIA alpha reference stack (v0.1.0) that wraps OpenClaw AI agents in hardened OpenShell sandboxes. Tech stack: TypeScript CLI + plugin, YAML blueprints/policies, Docker, k3s. Key dirs: src/lib/ (CLI core), nemoclaw/src/ (plugin), nemoclaw-blueprint/ (policies). Provides guided onboarding, deny-by-default network policies, credential isolation, and inference routing.

content: NemoClaw sits on top of OpenShell. It is the user-facing layer that orchestrates OpenShell underneath. Changes to OpenShell affect NemoClaw.

name: NemoClaw CLI Commands
description: Key commands: nemoclaw onboard (create sandbox), nemoclaw <name> connect (SSH in), nemoclaw <name> logs --follow (stream logs), nemoclaw <name> policy-add (add network preset), nemoclaw <name> policy-list, nemoclaw <name> destroy, nemoclaw credentials list/reset, nemoclaw start/stop (auxiliary services), nemoclaw status, nemoclaw debug, nemoclaw uninstall.

title: Enterprise web dashboard for NemoClaw/OpenShell
rationale: Target market is enterprise companies. Web-first approach chosen over desktop because enterprises need multi-user access, centralized control, SSO, RBAC, audit trails, and remote access. Architecture: React/Next.js frontend → REST/WebSocket API → OpenShell gRPC. Estimated 3-6 months for enterprise-grade. Not yet started — planning phase.

name: Input-to-UI Mapping
description: Complete mapping of all NemoClaw CLI inputs to web UI equivalents. Provider selection → card grid, API keys → password fields, messaging channels → toggle switches with nested config, policy presets → checkbox card grid. Post-onboard management maps to dashboard pages: sandbox list, detail, log viewer, embedded terminal (xterm.js), policy editor, credentials page.

title: 1-command deploy with web UI wizard
rationale: Replace interactive CLI onboard with 'nemoclaw deploy --ui'. Runs preflight+gateway automatically, opens browser to setup wizard on localhost:3000, wizard posts to /api/deploy which runs nemoclaw onboard non-interactively with env vars. Streams progress via SSE. After deploy, transitions to persistent dashboard. For enterprise, same server deployed persistently with SSO/RBAC.

name: NemoClaw Input Inventory
description: ~30 unique user inputs across onboard flow: provider selection (10 options), API key (password), model selection (per-provider lists), custom endpoint URL, 3 messaging channel toggles with nested config (tokens, IDs, mention mode), Brave web search toggle + key, sandbox name, policy preset selection (12 presets). ~15 CLI flags across management commands. Non-interactive mode driven by env vars: NEMOCLAW_NON_INTERACTIVE, NEMOCLAW_PROVIDER, NEMOCLAW_MODEL, NEMOCLAW_ENDPOINT_URL, NEMOCLAW_POLICY_MODE, NEMOCLAW_POLICY_PRESETS, plus credential env vars.

title: Enterprise features build order: SSO → RBAC → Multi-tenant
rationale: SSO first (auth foundation, use NextAuth.js/Auth0, integrate Okta/Azure AD/Google Workspace/SAML/OIDC). RBAC second (roles: Viewer, Operator, Admin, Security, Billing — permissions table + middleware). Multi-tenant last (team isolation: separate sandboxes, credentials, policies, cost tracking per team). This order works because each layer builds on the previous — RBAC needs auth, multi-tenant needs both.

name: Enterprise RBAC Role Definitions
description: 5 roles defined for NemoClaw web UI: Viewer (read sandbox status/logs), Operator (create sandboxes/connect/manage policies), Admin (full access — providers/credentials/destroy), Security (view/edit policies + audit logs only), Billing (cost reports only). Follows principle of least privilege. Needs permissions table in DB + middleware on every API call.

name: Multi-tenant Architecture
description: Each tenant (team/department) gets isolated: sandboxes, credentials (own API keys), policies (own security level), cost tracking, and user lists. Tenant-scoped DB queries required. UI needs tenant switcher for admins. Data isolation is a legal/compliance requirement. Single deployment serves all teams. Heaviest enterprise feature to build.

title: 1-prompt setup: single form with 2 required fields
rationale: Simplest possible onboarding. User runs 'nemoclaw deploy', browser opens to single-page form with only 2 required fields: provider dropdown and API key. Everything else (model, sandbox name, policies, messaging, web search) has smart defaults and is collapsed under Advanced. One Deploy button. Replaces 8-step CLI wizard. Minimum viable input: provider + API key.

name: Port forward dies periodically
description: OpenShell port forward for the OpenClaw dashboard (port 18789) dies after some time. Requires running 'openshell forward start 18789 <sandbox-name> --background' to restart. Web UI now includes a Start/Restart Forward button that calls this command via /api/forward endpoint.

title: Adopting OpenShell v0.0.57 needs NemoClaw integration work, not just a blueprint pin bump
rationale: v0.0.57 changed the gateway auth model: docker sandboxes require a gateway-minted sandbox JWT (configure [openshell.gateway.gateway_jwt] with Ed25519 signing_key_path, public_key_path, kid_path, ttl_secs), and enabling it also turns on user-request auth (needs [openshell.gateway.auth] allow_unauthenticated_users=true or real auth). NemoClaw currently bootstraps with OPENSHELL_DISABLE_GATEWAY_AUTH=true (NemoClaw/src/lib/onboard/docker-driver-gateway-env.ts), which v0.0.57 rejects. Also: the sandbox image must ship iproute2 or netns creation fails; bump min/max_openshell_version off 0.0.39 in the blueprint; re-validate install-openshell.sh feature checks. The runtime fix itself is verified working in an isolated v0.0.57 stack.

description: Running an isolated OpenShell test gateway (separate port, DB, and docker-network) on the SAME host Docker daemon as a live NemoClaw deployment is NOT actually isolated: the test gateway enumerates the shared Docker daemon, ADOPTS the production sandbox container into its own state, and on test-gateway shutdown its cleanup handler STOPS that container. This took the live Diffract demo down during a v0.0.57 evaluation.
resolution: Give the test gateway its OWN Docker daemon (separate DOCKER_HOST/dockerd) or a throwaway VM/host. Recovery: docker start brings the killed container back (agent Ready, inference OK) but stale /tmp blocks the chat backend; a clean recreate via nemoclaw <name> rebuild (needs NVIDIA_API_KEY in env) or a fresh deploy/onboard fixes it.

description: Live-patching the in-container Hermes TUI/Python (overlaying this repo newer ui-tui or tui_gateway source onto the pinned-image Hermes) breaks the TUI-to-gateway protocol and the chat ends immediately with 'session ended'. Same version-skew class as the host CLI vs deployed sandbox image (e.g. the recover guard #2478).
resolution: Bake version-matched branding into the sandbox image instead of live-patching (Riaan, commits 360270a/9f3df3d/a5c2d6f: new NemoClaw/agents/hermes/Dockerfile + agents/hermes/branding/). Only the self-contained web dashboard (web_dist) is safe to overlay live.
